title: Why is Komm Susser Todd in English
display_title: Why is Komm Susser Todd in English
wikitext:
[]: Why is Komm Susser Todd in English?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:38 am Apparently the song is in English, even in the Japanese-language version of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:59 am Because it's alluding to this. What I always found more baffling was the fact that they didn't manage to spell it correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:29 am You need to URL encode the non-ASCII characters in [url] tags for them to work; and the double-s character is obsolete in German orthography and has been for quite some years.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:33 am ^ Eh? check your facts... In standard spelling (since the orthography reform from 1996), ß is used only after long vowels and diphthongs, while ss is written after short vowels. [...] However, it is not used in all German-speaking countries, in particular not in Switzerland and Liechtenstein. German speakers in Germany, Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Luxembourg and South Tyrol, Italy follow the standard rules for ß.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:36 am I shall have to dig up the post where I'd used the double-s character and got corrected by a native.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jul 10, 2014 10:45 am Mr. Tines wrote: I shall have to dig up the post where I'd used the double-s character and got corrected by a native.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 10, 2014 12:55 pm I love a good language fight.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jul 10, 2014 2:21 pm Mr. Tines wrote: You need to URL encode the non-ASCII characters in [url] tags for them to work; and the double-s character is obsolete in German orthography and has been for quite some years.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 10, 2014 2:34 pm It's the "depends on the country" bit that I was expecting -- much like US and UK vary in how to spell words.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Jul 10, 2014 2:40 pm robersora wrote: I don't know what "URL encode" means. And I'm not entirely sure what a non-ASCII character is either.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jul 10, 2014 6:12 pm ^ Thank you!

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Jul 10, 2014 6:48 pm Why is it in english? Well I believe for the same reason Anno used the Christian Aesthetics, Rule of cool. But it could also serve several other reasons, alienating the japenese viewer while appealing to the western viewer.

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:58 pm robersora wrote: Because it's alluding to this. What I always found more baffling was the fact that they didn't manage to spell it correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 10, 2014 11:47 pm I wonder if, either intentionally or unintentionally, the English lyrics added another layer of confusion to the cognitive dissonance of that scene for the intended Japanese audience.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 11, 2014 12:29 am Whatever the reason I'm glad it is.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Jul 11, 2014 2:38 am I just remember reading somewhere that Anno wrote the lyrics which were then loosely translated into English because they wanted the movie to be released overseas in a big fashion. Maybe they were planning to produce the new Akira or Ghost in the Shell, or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Jul 11, 2014 11:51 am Isn't English one most spoken languages in the world only behind Chinese Mandarin? So having a song sung in a language that the many, many people will understand makes it's easier for Anno to express himself with-out it getting lost in translation.
summary:
[]: Why is Komm Susser Todd in English?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:38 am Apparently the song is in English, even in the Japanese-language version of the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Jul 10, 2014 8:59 am Because it's alluding to this. What I always found more baffling was the fact that they didn't manage to spell it corr
